libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: For version 2.0.0, generated tests must use jakarta.activation.* imports and class names, not javax.activation.*. Existing 1.2.1 tests and metadata-package filters using javax.activation need package migration for this version.

deterministic_setup.reason: The resolved jakarta.activation-api POM states it is intended for compilation and omits implementation-specific classes; artifact bytecode for MailcapCommandMap and MimetypesFileTypeMap references com.sun.activation.registries classes provided by the implementation jar.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8769 [Automation] javac compile fails for jakarta.activation:jakarta.activation-api:2.0.0; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: com.sun.activation:jakarta.activation:2.0.0 also contains jakarta.activation API classes, so tests should still intentionally exercise the target API behavior and avoid relying on implementation-only com.sun.activation types unless needed to enable normal runtime paths.

Some narrow API tests, such as CommandInfo bean instantiation or SecuritySupport resource access, may work without the implementation dependency, but broader Activation usage involving MIME type or mailcap resolution will not.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: jakarta.activation-api:2.0.0 is an API/compile artifact; normal runtime paths such as default CommandMap/FileTypeMap use com.sun.activation.registries classes that are supplied by the matching implementation artifact.
